market_summary: S&P 500 closed at a new all-time high of 7,230.12 on May 1 (+0.9% for the week ending May 1); Nasdaq hit 25,114.44 (+1.1%), also a record. April was the best month since 2020. Tech sector is re-asserting leadership with AI monetization entering revenue recognition phase. Strait of Hormuz dual blockade remains fully intact — US blockading Iranian ports, Iran restricting transit; Trump scrapped Islamabad peace talks. GLD 4-week invalidation clock has NOT started. Goldman Sachs raised Brent forecast to $90/barrel by late 2026. FOMC held 3.50-3.75% on April 29 in historic 8-4 vote (most divided since 1992); Governor Miran dissented for 25bps cut. Powell chairmanship ends May 15; Warsh expected to chair June 16-17 FOMC. Markets pricing zero cuts for all of 2026. April CPI due May 12 projected ~3.6% YoY (vs March +3.3%).
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ (DOMINANT): Dual blockade confirmed — ship transits collapsed 95% (130/day to 6/day). Trump scrapped Islamabad peace talks. GLD macro thesis fully intact; 4-week invalidation clock NOT started. 2. STAGFLATION RISK: March CPI +3.3% YoY; April projected +3.6%; WTI briefly above $110. Fed constrained — markets pricing zero 2026 cuts. Goldman raises Brent to $90 year-end. 3. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: AMZN Q1 validated hyperscaler AI spend — $44.2B quarterly capex, $200B FY2026 total; Bedrock tokens Q1 > all prior years combined; Amazon chips $20B+ annual run rate. NVDA May 20 earnings is next major market catalyst. 4. TECH LEADERSHIP RESTORED: Tech reclaiming sector leadership; Energy pulled back -1.45% this week. AI monetization showing up in earnings (AMZN, Google). Rotation back to growth from value/energy. 5. FED LEADERSHIP TRANSITION: Powell exits May 15; Warsh era begins June 16-17 FOMC — policy uncertainty elevated for H2 2026.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom is the highest-conviction new-position candidate. AI custom silicon leadership via Google TPU and Meta AI accelerator partnerships through 2029-2031; $73B+ AI backlog; Q1 2026 AI semiconductor revenue +106% YoY to $8.4B; Q2 guidance +47% YoY revenue growth; VMware networking provides durable recurring revenue. 44% Strong Buy / 48% Buy consensus, mean target ~$485. Zero effective WHT (negligible dividend). Complementary to NVDA (custom XPUs vs standard GPUs). Gate: NAV must exceed $5,000 — current NAV $4,321.08 is $678.92 below threshold. Do NOT initiate position yet.

action_items: 1. CRITICAL — Execute NOW (May 4-5): Add $150 into NVDA at market. May 20 earnings 16 days away. Source: $75 from U03A stage-down + $75 from available cash. Highest-conviction trade in portfolio.
2. CRITICAL — Execute NOW (May 4-5): Sell ~0.70 shares U03A (~$75) at market. First stage-down tranche. Proceeds fund NVDA add. Reduces U03A from 13.92% toward 10% NAV target.
3. CRITICAL — Execute NOW (May 4-5): Reduce MA by ~$75 (~0.15 shares at $495.46). April cross-border +9% YoY approaching DETERIORATING escalation trigger. Redeploy proceeds into CSPX.

4. EXECUTE — This week: Exit MO (0.0083 shares ~$0.62), NLY (0.2192 shares ~$5.00), SPY (0.0008 shares ~$0.58). Total ~$6.20. All carry 30% WHT with no thesis. SPY proceeds into CSPX; NLY/MO into cash buffer.
5. DEPLOY — After actions 2-4: Redeploy MA reduce proceeds + exit proceeds (~$81.80) into CSPX to maintain core anchor weight.
6. SET ALERT — GLD $420 daily close: Buffer is only $3.18 (0.75%). If GLD closes at or below $420 on any session, immediately trim 25% of GLD (~$106) into CSPX. Zero discretion permitted.

7. MONITOR — May 12: April CPI release (projected ~3.6% YoY). If above 3.8%: GLD bullish support, use any equity selloff as NVDA/CSPX add. If below 3.2%: risk-on may pressure GLD toward $420 trigger — be ready to act.

8. MONITOR — May 20: NVDA Q1 FY2027 earnings after market close. Thesis intact if revenue >=76B and Vera Rubin H2 ramp reaffirmed. Reassess conviction if HBM cuts exceed 40% with explicit Q2 revenue guide-down on the call.

9. WATCH — MA escalation: Q2 2026 earnings (late July) is the definitive test. If any May/June cross-border data falls below 8% YoY, escalate MA to DETERIORATING and evaluate full exit (~$279 into CSPX).
10. AVGO gate: NAV must reach $5,000 before initiating $300 position. Current NAV $4,321.08 is $678.92 below threshold. NVDA add + market appreciation could close gap within 4-8 weeks — monitor.
